ProductCreneau.name: Créneau
ProductCreneau.tagline: The marketplace digitizing Dakar driving schools.
ProductCreneau.description: A single platform for driving schools: student management, instructors, schedules, payments — and a public marketplace that automatically brings in new signups.
ProductCreneau.problem: The typical driving school keeps books in a notebook, students on WhatsApp, schedules in the manager's head. No visibility, lots of leaks, zero digital acquisition.
ProductCreneau.solution: Centralized dashboard for the manager, autonomous student space, dedicated marketplace exposing each driving school and converting prospects into direct signups.
ProductCreneau.feature1: Centralized management of students, instructors, schedules, payments
ProductCreneau.feature2: Public marketplace exposing each registered school
ProductCreneau.feature3: Direct signup link from each school's ad
ProductCreneau.feature4: Audience generated by TerangaDev side (SEO + acquisition)

ProductNurapic.name: Nurapic
ProductNurapic.tagline: Your event photos, found with a selfie.
ProductNurapic.description: The photographer uploads their thousands of event photos. Guests scan their face with their phone and AI instantly returns photos featuring them — which they can buy online.
ProductNurapic.problem: An event photographer takes 500 to 2,000 photos per night. To sell them, they have to find each guest one by one. Result: most photos are never sold.
ProductNurapic.solution: AI face recognition + public link shared by the photographer + unified guest interface. Guests find themselves and buy their photos directly online.
ProductNurapic.feature1: Unlimited upload on the photographer side
ProductNurapic.feature3: Guest interface: find all your photos across every event
ProductNurapic.feature4: Online payment · Wave, Orange Money, card

ProductSama.name: Sama Reservation
ProductSama.tagline: The hair, massage and beauty salon without the ringing phone.
ProductSama.description: A platform for salons (hair, massage, aesthetics) that digitizes appointment booking, staff management, and the calendar — while clients book online in a few clicks.
ProductSama.problem: The typical salon spends half its day answering the phone for appointments. Double bookings are frequent, cancellations unmanaged, and empty slots impossible to fill.
ProductSama.solution: Centralized agenda by staff member, online booking 24/7, automated reminders, and a dashboard to optimize occupancy.
ProductSama.feature1: 24/7 online booking by service and staff

ProductTicket.name: Ticket-Events
ProductTicket.tagline: Web ticketing for African concerts, conferences and trade shows.
ProductTicket.description: An event ticketing platform built for the African market: Wave / Orange Money / card payment, QR tickets, mobile access control, and self-service organizer management.
ProductTicket.problem: Event organizers in Senegal juggle Eventbrite (poorly suited to local payments), Excel sheets and WhatsApp. Access control is manual, fraud is common.
ProductTicket.solution: Self-service platform where the organizer creates their event in 10 minutes, sells tickets through all African payment channels, and scans QR codes at the entrance with their phone.
ProductTicket.feature2: Payment via Wave, Orange Money, bank card
ProductTicket.feature3: Secure QR tickets + mobile scanner for access control

CaseHydrautech.name: Hydrautech
CaseHydrautech.scope: Marketing site · management system · team training · maintenance
CaseHydrautech.tagline: We replaced the stock notebook with a platform. 200+ items tracked in under 2 months.
CaseHydrautech.description: Hydrautech is a Dakar-based industrial SME specialized in supplying and maintaining hydraulic equipment for Senegalese industry. Over 10 employees, dozens of active sites, and — before us — zero digital visibility on stock or operations.
CaseHydrautech.brief: Hydrautech's manager came to us with a simple goal: get out of paper-based management. He wanted a professional marketing site for the brand and a management system that would finally give him visibility on equipment, stock and teams — without imposing a brutal change on staff who weren't computer-savvy.
CaseHydrautech.challenge: Everything lived on paper or in the manager's head: equipment in/out tracked in a notebook, breakdowns reported by phone, invoices written by hand. Each error cost hours of searching, and impossible to answer a client in real time on equipment availability. The risk of silent losses was huge.
CaseHydrautech.approach: We scoped the project in two phases: first a modern marketing site to establish the brand, then a custom back-office to digitize management. The system had to be readable by employees who had never used management software — so simplified interface, no jargon, and 2 days of on-site training per role (manager, stock keeper, technician).
CaseHydrautech.phase1Body: One week on site to understand real flows — how each item enters, exits, returns, gets repaired. We mapped 4 distinct roles and 12 critical use cases.
CaseHydrautech.phase2Body: Visual identity reworked, responsive site in French, hydraulic catalog product sheets, quote form, local SEO. Shipped in 3 weeks.
CaseHydrautech.phase3Body: Filament back-office on top of Laravel + PostgreSQL. Equipment inventory with physical QR codes, intervention tracking, low stock alerts, accounting exports.
CaseHydrautech.phase4Body: 2 days of on-site training for the 10+ employees. Annual maintenance contract: security updates, quarterly evolutions, N1 and N2 support.
CaseHydrautech.stack: Laravel 12 · PostgreSQL · Filament · Docker · OVH France · Datamatrix QR codes

CaseNdougalma.name: Ndougalma
CaseNdougalma.scope: Ideation · business model · platform · digital marketing · maintenance
CaseNdougalma.tagline: An idea for a grocery shopping app. Live 2 months ahead of the original plan.
CaseNdougalma.description: A Senegalese entrepreneur came to us with an idea: let Senegalese people do their grocery shopping online, prioritizing local merchants. No existing platform, no marketing strategy, no formalized business model. Everything to build.
CaseNdougalma.brief: The brief was a single sentence: "I want to launch an app that would allow Senegalese to do their grocery shopping online." The client knew their market but had neither product experience nor technical expertise to execute. They were looking for a partner to take charge of the entire project — from idea to first euro earned.
CaseNdougalma.challenge: An idea isn't a product. We needed to validate the business model (average basket, margins, delivery logistics), define the precise target, choose an MVP that generates revenue without costing a fortune to maintain, and build marketing traction before public launch.
CaseNdougalma.approach: We laid out a 4-phase roadmap over 5 months — which we ultimately compressed to 3 months thanks to client availability and clear scoping from day one. Modern stack (Next.js + local payment Wave + Stripe for diaspora), light backend to iterate fast, and a digital marketing track running in parallel with technical build so go-live wasn't an empty page.
CaseNdougalma.phase1Body: Three 2-hour workshops on average basket, initial targeting, delivery logistics, and product margin. Output: a testable BM and a 6-month roadmap.
CaseNdougalma.phase2Body: Responsive Next.js web app with catalog, cart, checkout, user accounts, Wave + Orange Money + card payment. Deliberately light to iterate fast.
CaseNdougalma.phase3Body: Visual identity, social media launch, paid teasing campaign before go-live, partnerships with Dakar grocery micro-influencers.
CaseNdougalma.phase4Body: Production on OVH France, client training on admin, quarterly maintenance and evolution contract, performance monitoring.
CaseNdougalma.stack: Next.js 15 · PostgreSQL · Stripe · Wave Money · Orange Money · OVH France · Sentry
